arts.ttl: Tottenham: Djed Spence out to silence Mohamed Salah again and then earn England call-up
arts.sum: The full-back kept the Liverpool star quiet in the Carabao Cup semi-final first leg

arts.ttl: Djed Spence looks to silence Mo Salah again as Tottenham star sets himself big goal
arts.sum: Djed Spence is making up for lost time with a string of impressive performances for Tottenham during Ange Postecoglou's never-ending injury crisis - and now the defender is eyeing an England call

arts.ttl: Tottenham defender Dragusin suffers ACL injury
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur defender Radu Dragusin will miss the rest of the season with an anterior cruciate ligament injury.

arts.ttl: Irish teenager Melia in 'record' transfer to Spurs
arts.sum: Teenage forward Mason Melia to join Tottenham from St Patrick's Athletic next year for a "record transfer fee for a League of Ireland player".

arts.ttl: OFFICIAL: Tottenham complete record-breaking Mason Melia transfer
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur have officially completed the signing of St Patrick’s Athletic forward Mason Melia for a record-breaking transfer fee. Following confirmation of Melia’s move to Tottenham, St Pat’s sporting director Ger O’Brien told the club’s website : “We are delighted to agree a deal with Tottenham Hotspur for Mason Melia.

arts.ttl: ‘An unbelievable finisher’: why Mathys Tel is a perfect fit for Spurs
arts.sum: Often a victim of his own versatility, emerging from Harry Kane’s shadow at Bayern should allow Tel’s talent to shine “It’s very rare that this many clubs call you for a player,” Bayern Munich’s sporting director, Max Eberl, told Kicker last week when talking about the options available to Mathys Tel. It spoke not just of widespread recognition among Europe’s elite clubs of the 19‑year‑old’s quality but of a shift of policy in the Bundesliga leaders’ camp; from shutting their ears to all approaches at the beginning of January to accepting that leveraging a financial advantage out of the situation was the best they could do. A month-long transfer window leaves room for plenty of changes of heart and mind. Some will snort that Tottenham were a mere backup choice after Tel declined their initial approach, that the forward blinked when faced with the prospect of a second half of the Bundesliga season like his first, given a mere 253 minutes of playing time. More probable is that an especially reflective young man took time to think of what would work best for him rather than what would most suit Bayern. There is little contradictory about the process when you consider Tel is an explosive attacking weapon with the maturity to constantly adapt. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: “Adapting was complicated” – Tottenham star reflects on protection from referees, hails English football culture
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur have struggled in the absence of Guglielmo Vicario this season. The Italian picked up a long-term ankle injury back in November and since then, Spurs have had instability in goal. New signing Antonin Kinsky and Brandon Austin have played this season but they still feel the absence of Vicario. arts.ttl: Jamie Carragher reveals the Liverpool player he 'isn't the biggest fan of' - as he names the Premier League star he would replace him with
arts.sum: Liverpool's achievements have been all the more remarkable given they didn't sign a single player in the summer, while no further additions arrived in January either.
